_meta.description: Volume metrics section registered input nodes. These user-editable values define air tightness and building perimeter parameters. ACH@50Pa and NRL50 are C-classified (model-specific, different for Target vs Reference), while perimeter is G-classified (shared geometry).

instances.label: Air Changes per Hour at 50 Pa
instances.note: Blower door test result. NBC 9.36 default = 3.5 ACH@50Pa if no test performed.

instances.label: Normalized Leakage Rate at 75 Pa
instances.note: Commercial air tightness metric (Part 3 buildings). Expressed per unit envelope area.

instances.label: Building Perimeter
instances.note: Perimeter length of the building footprint. Used for slab edge loss calculations and envelope area computation.

signature.inputs: These ARE the inputs (registered via graph.registerInput in VolumeMetricsNodes.js)
signature.output: Values stored in MultiModelState, consumed by volume, envelope area, infiltration, and ventilation calculations

preconditions.description: Air changes per hour cannot be negative
Normalized leakage rate cannot be negative

behavior.output: Triggers recomputation of flow rate at 50Pa (h_106), leakage area (d_106), natural ACH (d_104), volumetric infiltration rate (h_104)
behavior.note: C-classified, model-specific. Key air tightness metric for Part 9 residential.

behavior.output: Triggers recomputation of infiltration annual rate (h_103) and per-area rate (g_103_calc)
behavior.note: C-classified, model-specific. Key air tightness metric for Part 3 commercial.

behavior.output: Triggers recomputation of envelope total area (d_102) and downstream surface-to-volume ratio (g_101)

edgeCases.handling: Flow rate = 0, leakage area = 0, natural ACH = 0
edgeCases.rationale: Perfectly sealed building (theoretical). Ventilation still operates mechanically.

edgeCases.handling: Envelope area = 2 × floorArea (no walls), surface-to-volume ratio based only on floor/ceiling
edgeCases.rationale: Edge case for underground or fully interior spaces

references.source: VolumeMetricsNodes.js (computed nodes consume these inputs for volume, envelope, and infiltration)
